title: Is Subsequence
description:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `s` is a **subsequence** of
`t`, and `false` otherwise.

A subsequence is formed by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from `t`
without changing the order of the remaining characters.
constraints: s and t consist only of lowercase letters.

examples.explanation: The empty string is a subsequence of anything.

solutions.summary: Advance through `s` only when its current char matches the scan of `t`.

steps.title: Track progress through s
steps.explanation: Keep an index `i` into `s` — it counts how many characters of `s` we have matched so far.

steps.title: Scan t and advance on a match
steps.explanation: Walk every character `c` of `t`; whenever it equals `s[i]`, move `i` forward. If `i` reaches the end, all of `s` was found in order.
